narrative.summary: Hanoi-born Vietnamese coffee brand. Military-green aesthetic, reclaimed wood, vintage memorabilia — anti-clean-girl café. Signature Bac Xiu (coconut white coffee) and coconut green rice smoothie. 4.5★ (582 reviews). Global chain but culturally interesting. Worth a deliberate stop for John’s design/architecture/art/food radar.

blocks.title: The Space
blocks.body: You're on the Annex stretch of Bloor, where the student bars thin out and the bookstores linger. Inside, it's all military green paint, reclaimed wood benches, and Vietnamese war-era propaganda posters layered with vintage enamel signage—deliberate, moody, nothing like the millennial-pink wave that hit coffee shops a few years back. The aesthetic leans into nostalgia for a 1940s Hanoi you've probably never seen, and it works because they commit fully to the bit.

blocks.title: The Food
blocks.body: This is Hanoi coffee-shop culture transplanted: strong, sweet Vietnamese coffee built on French colonial foundations, not third-wave pour-overs. The signature Bac Xiu flips the usual cà phê sữa ratio—mostly coconut milk cut with a shot of espresso, served over ice—and the coconut green rice smoothie tastes like neither a smoothie nor a dessert, somewhere in between with chewy rice grains at the bottom. It's a café menu, not a meal: banh mi, some sweets, but you're here for the drinks. Expect to spend under $10 for coffee and a snack.

blocks.title: Go When
blocks.body: Go mid-afternoon when you need to reset between meetings or gallery visits, or when you want to sit somewhere that feels designed without feeling precious. It's better as a deliberate stop than a grab-and-go—the kind of place where the interior is half the reason you came.

blocks.title: The Vibe
blocks.body:
⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Cong was recommended to me by one of my favourite Toronto baristas. He had spent some time in Vietnam and had been to a few of these franchised coffee shops when he was there. It was an absolutely amazing find once again proving that if you wanna know where the best coffee in Toronto is ask your barista

From the moment you stepped through the doors of Cong you realize that you are in a carefully curated and extremely lovingly designed space. Immediately when you walk in the door, there is a poster on the wall discussing the founder of the franchise and her vision for the cafés. I feel her vision has been fully realized. I have included a picture of this poster in my photos so that you can read the story of the café and the founders vision.

As many of you who follow my Google reviews will know, I do not generally like sweet coffee. My typical coffee drink is a straight up old school Cortodo  made with a very dark espresso roast. However, at Cong I asked the barista what she would recommend as the most typical coffee which would be served in the morning at the cafés in Vietnam. She produced an absolutely beautiful, frothy, sweet coffee, which I understand to be a combination of very strong espresso with condensed milk. The coffee is somehow frothed to make it beautifully rich with just the perfect amount of sweetness.

We are so absolutely fortunate in Toronto to have such a vast variety of coffee experiences open to us .Cong is a perfect example of the way in which the world of coffee is now easily accessible in Toronto. I certainly will be back to this beautiful café to try some of their other signature coffee drinks. This is an absolute must coffee experience if you're in the Annex!"

⭐⭐⭐⭐ "Follow @bredgyeats for more food posts!

Baked to a crispy crunch, the bread itself was aromatic and yeasty with a soft, fluffy interior. The trio of classic cold cuts, subtly savory pate, and a rich, but not overpowering butter brings the mouth-watering flavour, while the fresh cilantro, cucumbers and finely chopped pickled carrots offer a refreshing contrast and a subtly sweet acidity. Pair it with something sweet and creamy, like the Coconut Coffee, which has a fine, icy texture of coconut milk with swirls of creamy Vietnamese coffee."

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐ "I & my bf came in earlier today to grab coffee and sandwiches. The bac xiu & nau brown were flavourful; Banh mi was spot on  as well. The manager girl who served us was so friendly and energetic despite it was super early in the morning 👍 highly recommend for anyone who wants to experience authentic Viet coffee!"
